title: -Amendment to Contract No. 32100160: Alameda-Contra Costa County Transit District (AC Transit) For EasyPass Program 2021-2025

sections.recommendation:
Adopt a Resolution ratifying the action taken by the City Manager during recess
to amend Contract No. 32100160 to cover expenses for the remainder of 2025 for
employee transit pass program with an additional $125,086 for a new total amount not
to exceed $899,539.
NEGATIVE EFFECT IF ACTION IS DELAYED UNTIL AFTER COUNCIL RECESS
If delayed until after recess, the required timeline for the City’s internal contract
execution would exceed the deadline to remit payment to AC Transit to ensure ongoing
coverage of employees utilizing this commuter benefit. Non-payment would lead to
additional car trips by City staff, additional congestion around City facilities and would
impact available public parking in areas surrounding City facilities.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
Currently, $35,869 is available in Contract No. 32100160 which had an original not to
exceed amount of $774,453. The additional $125,086 is available in the General Fund
Payroll Deduction Trust Fund. This same fund has historically covered this expense.
sections.current_situation:
The City and AC Transit formed a partnership to establish an employee transit pass
program in 2002 to provide all eligible City employees unlimited rides on all AC Transit
buses. Delay in payment may result in program disruption.
By continuing to provide this employee benefit program, the City remains a model
employer for Berkeley businesses required to offer a Commuter Benefit Program
(Berkeley Municipal Code 9.88) or Trip Reduction Information Program.
The City’s contract with AC Transit was previously managed by Public Works, but as it
is a benefit provided to City staff, it is moving under the management of the Human

Contract No. 32100160 Amendment: Alameda-Contra Costa County
Transit District (AC Transit) For EasyPass Program 2021-2025 CONSENT CALENDAR
September 9, 2025
Resources Department. The amount reflected in this Resolution, corresponds to an
amount that had been previously billed to the City amid a staffing vacancy in Public
Works and serves to close the current contract, prior to execution of a new contract for
the next five-year period.
Maintaining funding for AC Transit’s Easy Pass for City staff is in alignment with the
City’s Strategic Plan Priorities, advancing the goal to be a global leader in addressing
climate change, advancing environmental justice, and protecting the environment, while
attracting and retaining a talented and diverse City government workforce.
sections.background:
Council previously approved extension of the program since its inception in 2002, again
in 2015 (Resolution No. 66,888-N.S.) and in 2021 (Resolution 69,663-N.S.). While it is
customary for the City to extend Requests for Proposals to manage expenditures of this
size and duration, AC Transit is the sole public transit provider able to meet the program
requirement and the City must execute a contract directly with the public agency. A new
contract will be executed for future years, as this Amendment only covers the remainder
of 2025.
ENVIRONMENTAL SUSTAINABILITY AND CLIMATE IMPACTS
By maintaining the AC Transit EasyPass Program, the City is reducing vehicle miles
traveled in individual vehicles and reducing associated greenhouse gasses by
encouraging staff to utilize public transit.
sections.rationale: Without payment, there may be a disruption in service provided to City staff,
sections.alternatives:
There is not another public transit agency that can provide the associated services
within this contract. Nonpayment would cause a disruption in service for staff depending
on utilizing the EasyPass program for their workday commute. This is an ongoing
benefit provided to City staff, which staff rely on to commute to their associated City
workplaces.
CONTACT PERSON
Amanda Montez, Senior Management Analyst, Public Works, (510) 981-6317

Page 2

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
CONTRACT NO. 32100160 AMENDMENT: ALAMEDA-CONTRA COSTA COUNTY
TRANSIT DISTRICT (AC TRANSIT) FOR EASYPASS PROGRAM 2021 TO 2025
WHEREAS, in January 2002 the City and AC Transit formed a partnership to establish an
employee transit pass program to provide all eligible City employees unlimited rides on all
AC Transit buses; and
WHEREAS, the concentration of public bus service in Berkeley and the size of the City's
workforce support an employee pass program with the Alameda-Contra Costa Transit
District (AC Transit); and
WHEREAS, the EasyPass program is a proven and popular benefit encouraging use of
public transit for City employees, and contributing to reduction in the use of single
occupancy vehicles for work and recreational travel; and
WHEREAS, by continuing to provide this employee benefit program, the City remains a
model employer for Berkeley businesses required to offer a Commuter Benefit Program
(Berkeley Municipal Code 9.88) or Trip Reduction Information Program; and
WHEREAS, since forming a partnership in 2002 to establish the EasyPass (then EcoPass)
Program, the City of Berkeley and AC Transit has offered employees unlimited usage
passes for all AC Transit local and Transbay buses; and
WHEREAS, the Council has approved extension of the program since its inception in
2002, again in 2015 (Resolution No. 66,888-N.S.) and most recently in 2021 (Resolution
69,663-N.S.); and
WHEREAS, the funding for the contract period beginning in 2021 and ending in 2025 was
not sufficient to cover staff needs, which require an amendment to add $125,086 for a
new total amount not to exceed $899,539; and
WHEREAS, $35,869 is available in contract #32100160 which had an original not to
exceed amount of $774,453. An additional $125,086 is available in the General Fund
Payroll Deduction Trust Fund to cover the amount needed to remit full payment for the
2025 calendar year.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the Ci
